Pilosella officinarum is a perennial herb in the dandelion family (Asteraceae), characterized by its yellow flower heads and edible roots. The plant is valued for its health benefits, including its ability to support digestion, reduce inflammation, and act as a diuretic. The bioactive constituents of Pilosella officinarum include sesquiterpene lactones, such as pilosulin and pilosulide, as well as other compounds with potential medicinal properties. Herbal preparations made from the plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to support digestive health and alleviate symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome.
